title: rubus × sparsiaculeatus
intro: Rubus × sparsiaculeatus, also known as the thimbleberry, is a hybrid species of the Rubus genus, combining the characteristics of the thimbleberry (Rubus parviflorus) and the bramble (Rubus idaeus). The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Rubus × sparsiaculeatus include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce swelling and pain, and protect against cell damage caused by free radicals. Herbal preparations of Rubus × sparsiaculeatus can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, which are often used to treat minor injuries and skin conditions.
